note: bio/9.0: Memory consolidation and hippocampal replay — how the brain completes deferred/pending tasks during offline processing; analogy for phase 8 deferred item completion and working-memory notifications
bio/9.0: Hippocampal replay during sleep — replaying recent experiences to consolidate memory; analogy for re-emitting deferred notifications and catching up event subscribers

bio/9.0+9.3: Default Mode Network — self-referential background processing, introspection, narrative construction; analogy for background completion of deferred tasks and self-documenting architecture
bio/9.1: CNS immune privilege — how the brain enforces isolation from peripheral immune surveillance; analogy for module sandboxing, capability isolation, and policy-as-code enforcement

bio/9.1: Microglia as the brain's resident immune cells — pathogen recognition, phagocytosis, threat response, synaptic pruning; analogy for OPA policy agents continuously evaluating and enforcing access rules
bio/9.1: Blood-brain barrier — selective permeability, tight junctions as security boundaries; apoptosis of damaged cells; analogy for container sandboxing (gVisor) and OPA capability isolation
bio/9.1: Apoptosis (programmed cell death) — controlled self-destruction of compromised cells; analogy for container termination policies, sandboxed process isolation, and graceful pod eviction

bio/9.1: Pattern Recognition Receptors (PRRs, TLRs) — innate immune threat detection via molecular patterns; Danger Hypothesis (Matzinger); analogy for OPA policy rules matching request patterns against known threat signatures

bio/9.2: Adult neurogenesis in the hippocampus — new neurons deployed into existing circuits; radial glia scaffolding, cell migration, synaptic integration; analogy for deploying new module containers into the running cognitive stack
bio/9.2: Cortical columns (minicolumns, hypercolumns) — modular, self-contained functional units of cortex; barrel cortex; analogy for per-module Docker containers with defined interface boundaries

bio/9.2: Myelination — wrapping axons to accelerate signal transmission; saltatory conduction; analogy for optimized container networking, resource limits tuning, and HPA scaling for production performance

bio/9.2: Cerebral blood flow autoregulation — dynamic scaling of metabolic resources to active brain regions (neurovascular coupling); analogy for Kubernetes HPA — automatically scaling pods in response to CPU/memory demand

bio/9.2: Synaptic pruning — elimination of unused connections during development; Hebbian 'use it or lose it'; analogy for container resource limits, pod eviction, and removing unused service replicas

bio/9.3: Metacognition — the brain's capacity to think about its own thinking; self-monitoring, error detection, confidence estimation; analogy for self-referential documentation that describes how the system works from within

bio/9.3: Semantic memory — long-term storage of factual, conceptual knowledge; schema-like representations; analogy for documentation as a structured knowledge base that the system can reference about itself

bio/9.3: Hebbian learning ('neurons that fire together wire together') — activity-dependent strengthening of connections; analogy for documentation written alongside implementation solidifies understanding and cross-links

bio/9.3: Predictive coding (Friston) — the brain maintains a generative model of itself and the world, updating on prediction errors; analogy for documentation as a maintained predictive model of the codebase
tech/9.1: Open Policy Agent (OPA) — policy-as-code engine for authorization in microservices; Rego language; data-driven policy evaluation; sidecar and standalone deployment modes
tech/9.1: gVisor — application kernel providing container sandboxing; runsc runtime; KVM and ptrace modes; compatibility with Docker and Kubernetes containerd runtime class
tech/9.1: SPIFFE/SPIRE — workload identity framework for zero-trust service meshes; SVIDs (X.509 or JWT); automatic certificate rotation; mTLS bootstrapping for inter-service auth
tech/9.2: Docker multi-stage builds — minimize final image size; separate build and runtime stages; best practices for Python (uv) and Node.js services
tech/9.2: Kubernetes Deployment manifest spec — rolling updates, replicas, pod template, resource requests/limits, readiness/liveness probes
tech/9.2: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler — CPU/memory-based autoscaling; custom metrics via Prometheus adapter; scale-to-zero patterns
tech/9.1+9.2: Kubernetes NetworkPolicy — pod-level firewall rules; namespace isolation; ingress/egress control for inter-module communication security
tech/9.0: Server-Sent Events (SSE) subscription patterns — event stream reconnection, Last-Event-ID, retry intervals; patterns for resources/subscribe notifications over SSE
tech/9.0: W3C Trace Context spec — traceparent and tracestate header formats; making traceparent required in MCPContext schema; OpenTelemetry propagation conformance
tech/9.3: MkDocs Material theme — documentation-as-code; Markdown-based; search, cross-linking, versioning; suitable for EndogenAI docs site
tech/9.0+9.3: AsyncAPI v3 — machine-readable specification for event-driven APIs including SSE and WebSocket; auto-generating documentation for resources/subscribe notifications
tech/9.0: Lighthouse — automated web performance, accessibility, best practices, SEO auditing; Lighthouse CI for automated score enforcement; achieving ≥90 score target
tech/9.1: OWASP Container Security — non-root containers, read-only filesystems, seccomp/AppArmor profiles, image scanning with Trivy/Grype, container best practices for production AI services
